attributes.description: Alerts when `haproxy.responses.average_time > 500 ms`. Backend application latency directly degrades user experience.
attributes.name: [HAProxy OTel] High backend response time

investigation_guide.blob:
## HAProxy high backend response time

### What fired
`haproxy.responses.average_time > 500 ms` during the window.

### Why it matters
Response time measures the interval from connection to first response byte — the actual application processing latency. Regressions here usually reflect application or dependency issues.

### Triage
1. Correlate with application metrics / traces on the backend.
2. Check error and retry counters on the same backend.
3. Inspect DB/cache latency from the backend.

### Remediation
- Fix the slow application or dependency.
- Scale backends if the workload has grown.

### Tuning
- 500 ms is a generic threshold; tune to SLA.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-haproxyreceiver.otel-*
// Response time (first byte from server) at backend/server level
| WHERE haproxy.responses.average_time IS NOT NULL
| STATS max_rtime = MAX(haproxy.responses.average_time)
  BY haproxy.proxy_name, haproxy.service_name, haproxy.addr
// Threshold in ms — tune for your application SLA
| WHERE max_rtime > 500
| EVAL alert_key = CONCAT(TO_STRING(haproxy.proxy_name), "::", TO_STRING(haproxy.service_name), "::", COALESCE(TO_STRING(haproxy.addr), ""))
| SORT max_rtime DESC
| LIMIT 50
